scope.summary: Doctor RLS/VPD diagnostics now redact runtime-observed session identity before rendering detail, and the regression test requires the live session user to stay omitted.
out_of_scope: No live Oracle D3 fixture was run in this close; coverage is the doctor unit mock that exercises the RLS/VPD observation path.
No classifier, query-result RLS/VPD surfacing, or PL/SQL intelligence behavior was changed.

entry_points.name: focused RLS/VPD doctor redaction regression
entry_points.trace: CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/home/durakovic/projects/oraclemcp/target cargo test -p oraclemcp-core doctor_rls_vpd_visibility -- --nocapture exited 0; doctor_rls_vpd_visibility_names_visible_policy now asserts session_user=<redacted> and forbids ORACLEMCP_D3_SIGHTED / ORACLEMCP_D3_OWNER in the detail while preserving ORACLEMCP_D3_VPD.

entry_points.name: oraclemcp doctor RLS/VPD visibility check
entry_points.trace: run_doctor includes check_rls_vpd_visibility; the source commit routes the OracleVpdRlsObservation session context through sanitized_detail_with_extra_values before building the CheckResult detail.

limitations: The shared checkout had unrelated untracked files from other panes; evidence is scoped to the committed doctor.rs source change.
The source commit is 626ef612f76a52af0187a2d0549adc51cf72741e rather than current HEAD because another pane advanced HEAD before this close evidence was written.
